observations.recommendation: We recommend that the BAC will prepare the prescribed procurement monitoring report that would represent all procurement activities for each semester and submit the same to the GPPB pursuant to Section 12.2 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184, copy furnished to the Office of the Auditor.

We recommend the Management to submit the approved Annual Investment Plan
and submit regularly the Accomplishment Report on the utilization of 20% EDF
showing the category and details of the projects/activities financed under the
funds. Adhere strictly with DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-01
dated February 22, 2017. It is the responsibility of the Municipal Mayor to ensure
the optimal utilization in order to help achieve desirable socio-economic
development and environmental outcomes.

Instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to submit to the Audit
Team the abovementioned Disbursements Vouchers for verification.

The Management should instruct the Municipal General Services Officer to finish
the physical inventory of all its properties and prepare the Report on the Physical
Count of PPE (RPCPPE) and to coordinate with the Accounting office for
reconciliation of the records.
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant should submit bank reconciliation monthly..

The Management should instruct the Municipal General Services Officer to finish the physical inventory of all its properties and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) and to coordinate with the Accounting office for reconciliation of the records.

We recommend that the unexpended LDRRMF for the year end should be transferred to a special trust fund in relation to Section 21 of RA 10121, DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-73 dated April 17, 2012 and COA Circular No. 2012-002.
We recommend that the Disbursing Officer to observe that prompt and timely payment of the mandatory deductions of their organic employees.
Management should also implement some of the programs/activities for women during disaster, calamities and other crisis situations for their GAD programs.

The Management should utilize their 20% Development Fund in accordance    with the above mentioned Memorandum Circular and other pertinent laws.
The Management should also venture on other projects covered or Social Development.

We recommend that the Management should instruct its Local School Board,                         that during the planning and budget preparation for the ensuing year, consider and include also expenditures allocated for public school facilities and equipment; educational research; purchase of books and periodicals; and sports development;
Management should ensure that SEF be expended exclusively for expenditures provided for under joint circular no. 01 series of 1998 issued by DECS, DBM and DILG.

Management should require the Municipal General Services Officer to conduct physical inventory of all its properties and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) and to coordinate with the Accounting Office for the reconciliation of the records.

The Management should open a separate special trust fund account.
The unexpended LDRRMF for the year end should accrue to a special trust fund solely for the purpose of supporting disaster risk reduction and management activities of the LDRRMC within the next five (5) years.

We recommend that management should re-constitute a Municipal Solid Waste Management Board in conformity to the membership requirement under RA 9003;
We recommend that the Board to develop a Solid Waste Management Plan and adopt measures to promote and ensure the effective implementation of this plan and programs.

The management should integrate in their regular activities, programs and projects that enhance the mobility and safety of differently-abled persons and senior citizens in accordance with Section 32 of GAA.
Management should instruct the municipal accountant to submit monthly or at least quarterly, monitoring reports on the status of implementation of the fund.
Mngt should instruct the general services officer to conduct physical inventory of all its properties and prepare the report on the physical count of PPE.
observations.managementAction: Management conducted their physical inventory, however it was not finish.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower and poor organization of property records like ledgers and cards.

observations.recommendation: Mngt should submit a report on the implementation of their 20% Development Fund.
observations.managementAction: Management has submitted report and has incurred disbursement above the threshold 20% of the IRA.

observations.recommendation: a.) Management should verify the status of these checks and submit the corresponding disbursement vouchers.
observations.managementAction: Management has submitted the corresponding disbursement vouchers with the supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: a.) Management should submit a complete set of monthly reports.
observations.managementAction: Mngt have submitted the required report of collection and deposit together with the other required reports.

observations.recommendation: a.) Management should observe guidelines under the presentation of financial statements under the PPSAS.
observations.managementAction: Mngt has followed the guidelines under the PPSAS and uses the appropriate accounts.

observations.recommendation: Management should include programs for senior citizens and submit reports on the implementation.
observations.managementAction: Mngt has include programs for senior citizens and submit reports on the implementation.

observations.recommendation: a.) Management should provide the audit team a sufficient office space.
observations.managementAction: Management has provided the audit team a sufficient office space.
